findings.problem: After the classroom set-piece, the chapter's last movement mostly receives packets, interpretation, and incentives instead of forcing Nita or Ben through a fresh on-page choice. That leaves the ending as strong setup for the next chapter rather than a fully landed award-level chapter in its own right.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the apartment sequence beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:331, intensify the employer contact at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:371, and rework the final bundle beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:475 so one institution makes a concrete same-night ask tied to Leela, the notebook, school attendance, or media access, and Nita or Ben must materially refuse, hedge, lie, or comply on the page.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised sequence from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:331 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:507 contains at least one explicit action demand with immediate cost or benefit and one visible response by Nita or Ben before the last paragraph; FAIL if the scene still ends on packets, interpretation, and bonus offers without a new decision.

findings.problem: The final third repeats one institutional point too many times: the response kit, Pilar warning, Saskia call, euphemism checklist, and continuity upgrade all restate that both systems find the clip useful. Because the beats are so similar in meaning, dramatic momentum flattens into procedural reiteration.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or differentiate the post-school beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:333, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:343, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:371, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:417, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:475 so each one changes the pressure in a different way: dissemination threat, concrete extraction demand, and material leverage should not all be delivered as variants of the same insight.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each post-school intervention in the revised sequence from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:331 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:491 has a distinct page function that can be named by reading it: one names the threat, one imposes a concrete demand, and one changes material stakes; FAIL if any two consecutive interventions merely restate that the scandal is marketable or trust-positive.

findings.problem: The narrator repeatedly captions how the room's humor and discomfort land instead of trusting bodies and timing to carry the beat. In a comedy-pressurized chapter, this explanatory reaction-labeling deflates the joke pressure the style bible asks to arrive through juxtaposition rather than commentary.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the reaction-caption lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:35, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:105, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:133, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:153 so the room's response is shown through observable behavior, silence, or physical interruption rather than narrator verdicts about what kind of laugh or applause it was.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least two of the four cited reaction labels are replaced by concrete room behavior that lets the reader infer the social effect, and no replacement sentence explicitly tells the reader that a joke landed badly or that the wrong people laughed; FAIL if the revised passage still relies on narrator labeling for most of those beats.

findings.problem: Emphasis increasingly arrives through clipped fragments such as 'Not metaphorically. Literally.' and 'Not desire exactly. Not now.', which becomes a visible prose tic and brushes against the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the emphatic fragment beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:160, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:263, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:401 into grammatically complete sentences or fold them into adjacent sentences so the stress comes from diction and sentence pressure rather than omitted subjects or auxiliaries.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each cited beat is rewritten as a full sentence with an explicit subject and auxiliary, or absorbed into a neighboring full sentence without losing emphasis; FAIL if any of those emphasis beats still rely on clipped fragments with omitted function words.

summary: Dialogue is the strongest lens here: Nita, Ben, Leela, Saskia, and the school-administrator voices remain distinct, contraction use stays in bounds, and I found no canonical continuity contradiction, tense drift, example-line reuse, or sanitization retreat. The chapter still fails on award, craft, and prose because the apartment aftermath substitutes repeated institutional explanation for a harder active choice, then compounds that flattening with narrator-captioned reaction beats and a recurring clipped-fragment prose tic.
